import { EmbeddingModelV3 } from '../../embedding-model/v3/embedding-model-v3';
import { ImageModelV3 } from '../../image-model/v3/image-model-v3';
import { LanguageModelV3 } from '../../language-model/v3/language-model-v3';
import { RerankingModelV3 } from '../../reranking-model/v3/reranking-model-v3';
import { SpeechModelV3 } from '../../speech-model/v3/speech-model-v3';
import { TranscriptionModelV3 } from '../../transcription-model/v3/transcription-model-v3';

/**
 * Provider for language, text embedding, and image generation models.
 */
export interface ProviderV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';

  /**
   * Returns the language model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {LanguageModel} The language model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  languageModel(modelId: string): LanguageModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the text embedding model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {LanguageModel} The language model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  embeddingModel(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the text embedding model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {EmbeddingModel} The embedding model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   *
   * @deprecated Use `embeddingModel` instead.
   */
  textEmbeddingModel?(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the image model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {ImageModel} The image model associated with the id
   */
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the transcription model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {TranscriptionModel} The transcription model associated with the id
   */
  transcriptionModel?(modelId: string): TranscriptionModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the speech model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {SpeechModel} The speech model associated with the id
   */
  speechModel?(modelId: string): SpeechModelV3;

  /**
   * Returns the reranking model with the given id.
   * The model id is then passed to the provider function to get the model.
   *
   * @param {string} modelId - The id of the model to return.
   *
   * @returns {RerankingModel} The reranking model associated with the id
   *
   * @throws {NoSuchModelError} If no such model exists.
   */
  rerankingModel?(modelId: string): RerankingModelV3;
}

Now I see the issue. The `ProviderV3` interface has:
- `readonly specificationVersion: 'v3'`
- `languageModel(modelId: string): LanguageModelV3`
- `embeddingModel(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3`
- `imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3`

My `PixelArtProvider` interface extends `ProviderV3`, and the object literal I'm returning in `createPixelArt()` has `specificationVersion: 'v3'` - that should work.

Wait, the error says: `Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'specificationVersion' does not exist in type 'PixelArtProvider'`.


This is happening because TypeScript can't resolve the `ProviderV3` import, so my `PixelArtProvider` interface has no known properties. Once I fix the module paths, this should resolve. But looking at how the black-forest-labs-provider handles this, they're returning a plain object with the same structure, so my approach should work once the imports are correct.

40	   */
41	  fetch?: FetchFunction;
42	}
43	
44	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	// Provider interface
46	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47	
48	export interface PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3 {
49	  /**
50	   * Creates a PixelArt image generation model.
51	   *
52	   * @param modelId - The model ID to use (e.g. `"pixelart-v2"`).
53	   * @param settings - Optional model-level settings.
54	   */
55	  image(
56	    modelId: PixelArtImageModelId,
57	    settings?: PixelArtImageSettings,
58	  ): ImageModelV3;
59	
60	  /**
61	   * Creates a PixelArt image generation model.
62	   * Alias for `image()`.
63	   *
64	   * @param modelId - The model ID to use (e.g. `"pixelart-v2"`).
65	   * @param settings - Optional model-level settings.
66	   */
67	  imageModel(
68	    modelId: PixelArtImageModelId,
69	    settings?: PixelArtImageSettings,
70	  ): ImageModelV3;
71	}
72	
73	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	// Factory function
75	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	const DEFAULT_BASE_URL = 'https://api.pixelart.ai/v1';
78	
79	export function createPixelArt(
80	  options: PixelArtProviderSettings = {},
81	): PixelArtProvider {
82	  const baseURL =
83	    withoutTrailingSlash(options.baseURL ?? DEFAULT_BASE_URL) ??
84	    DEFAULT_BASE_URL;
85	
86	  const getHeaders = () =>
87	    withUserAgentSuffix(
88	      {
89	        Authorization: `Bearer ${loadApiKey({
90	          apiKey: options.apiKey,
91	          environmentVariableName: 'PIXELART_API_KEY',
92	          description: 'PixelArt',
93	        })}`,
94	        ...options.headers,
95	      },
96	      `ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}`,
97	    );
98	
99	  const createImageModel = (
100	    modelId: PixelArtImageModelId,
101	    settings: PixelArtImageSettings = {},
102	  ) =>
103	    new PixelArtImageModel(modelId, settings, {
104	      provider: 'pixelart.image',
105	      baseURL,
106	      headers: getHeaders,
107	      fetch: options.fetch,
108	    });
109	
110	  // PixelArt only supports image generation – throw for any other model type.
111	  const unsupportedLanguageModel = (modelId: string) => {
112	    throw new NoSuchModelError({ modelId, modelType: 'languageModel' });
113	  };
114	
115	  const unsupportedEmbeddingModel = (modelId: string) => {
116	    throw new NoSuchModelError({ modelId, modelType: 'embeddingModel' });
117	  };
118	
119	  return {
120	    specificationVersion: 'v3',
121	    image: createImageModel,
122	    imageModel: createImageModel,
123	    languageModel: unsupportedLanguageModel,
124	    embeddingModel: unsupportedEmbeddingModel,
125	  };
126	}
127	
128	/**
129	 * Default PixelArt provider instance.
130	 * Reads credentials from the `PIXELART_API_KEY` environment variable.
131	 */
132	export const pixelart = createPixelArt();

## PixelArt Provider — Summary

Created a new `@ai-sdk/pixelart` package at `packages/pixelart/` following the exact same patterns as existing providers like `@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs` (an image-only provider). Here's what was built:

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/pixelart/package.json` | Package config with `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` workspace deps, CJS + ESM exports |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript project config extending `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json` with composite builds |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json` | Build-time tsconfig (disables composite for tsup) |
| `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts` | Build config: CJS + ESM + type declarations + sourcemaps + version injection |
| `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts` | Package version constant injected at build time |
| `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` union type + `PixelArtImageSettings` interface |
| `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel` class implementing `ImageModelV3` |
| `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts` | `PixelArtProvider` interface + `createPixelArt()` factory + default `pixelart` instance |
| `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts` | Public API exports |

Also updated `tsconfig.json` (root) to add `packages/pixelart` to the project references.

### Architecture

- **`PixelArtProvider`** extends `ProviderV3` — only `image()` and `imageModel()` are functional; `languageModel()` and `embeddingModel()` throw `NoSuchModelError`
- **`PixelArtImageModel`** implements `ImageModelV3.doGenerate()` — posts JSON to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1/images/generations` and returns base64-encoded images
- **Provider-specific settings** (`size`, `style`, `pixelDensity`) are passed via `providerOptions.pixelart` using the standard `parseProviderOptions` pattern (same as black-forest-labs, openai, etc.)
- Auth uses `Bearer` token from `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var or explicit `apiKey` option
- `aspectRatio` generates a warning since PixelArt doesn't support it

### Usage
```typescript
import { pixelart, createPixelArt } from '@ai-sdk/pixelart';
import { generateImage } from 'ai';

const result = await generateImage({
  model: pixelart.image('pixelart-v2'),
  prompt: 'A medieval castle at sunset',
  providerOptions: {
    pixelart: {
      size: '512x512',
      style: 'pixel',
      pixelDensity: 4,
    },
  },
});
```
